lessons.title: What is your name?
lessons.topic: Identity and the sounds of F and G

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How do you greet your new friend?
questions.hint: It is something you say when you first see them.

questions.question_text: What sound does the name 'George' start with?
What word can you use to say goodbye?
questions.hint: It's what you say when you leave.

steps.title: Funny Goat
steps.text: Hello! My name is Anna. I am a girl. This is my goat. He is a funny goat. I have a fish too.

steps.explanation: Use 'This is my' to show what you have.

steps.audio_text: Hello! I am Tom. This is my friend, Faye. She likes her funny goat.

steps.prompt: Say: My name is [Your Name] and I have a goat.
steps.expected_text: my name is [your name] and i have a goat

questions.question_text: My name is _____ .
Which one is a 'goat'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: The word 'fish' starts with F.
Fill in the blank: My name is Ben. I am a _____.

lessons.title: He and She
lessons.topic: Gender pronouns and the sound of H

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What color is the balloon the girl is holding?
questions.hint: It's the color of an apple.

questions.question_text: What does the girl say when she sees the boy?
questions.hint: It's something you say to start a conversation.

questions.question_text: Who has a red balloon?

steps.title: The Park
steps.text: My name is Tom. He is Jack. He is my friend. She is Lily. She has a red hat. This is my favorite park.

steps.explanation: Use 'He' for boys and 'She' for girls.

steps.audio_text: This is Lucy. She is wearing a pink hat. Lucy is happy because she sees Tom.

questions.question: Who is Lucy happy to see?

questions.question_text: Who is wearing a hat?
Complete the sentence: He ___ happy.
What does 'he' refer to?
True or False: A 'she' can be a girl.
What sound does the word 'hat' start with?

lessons.title: Meet My Friend
lessons.topic: Introduction of others and sounds of I and J

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What sound does the letter 'I' make in the word 'igloo'?
What letter makes the 'juh' sound in 'jellybean'?
Which letter is the first sound in the word 'jump'?

steps.title: Leo and the Jet
steps.text: This is my friend. His name is Leo. He is happy in an igloo! Look at the jet! It flies fast.

steps.explanation: Use 'He is my' to introduce a boy.

steps.audio_text: Hi! I'm Isla. This is my friend Jack. We love playing in the igloo together.

questions.question: Where do Isla and Jack like to play?

questions.question_text: This is my ___. (Grammar question, fill the blank with 'friend', 'jet', or 'igloo')
Which word means a place made of ice? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: A 'jet' is an animal.
If Isla says, 'This is my friend.', who is she talking about? (Reading comprehension)
questions.correct_answer: Someone she likes spending time with.
options: Someone she likes spending time with.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What friendly animal lives in the water and starts with 'F'?
questions.hint: It has fins and swims all day.

questions.question_text: What fruit is yellow and starts with 'B'?
What round fruit comes in bunches and starts with 'G'?
questions.hint: It can be purple or green and is often made into juice.

steps.title: My Name is Ben
steps.text: Hello! My name is Ben. I am a boy. This is Lily. She is my friend. We see a frog and a cat.

questions.question: What do Ben and Lily see?

steps.audio_text: Hi, I'm Emma! My friend Jack loves playing soccer and eating apples. He is very fast!

questions.question: What sport does Jack love to play?
What fruit does Jack like to eat?

steps.prompt: Say: My name is Emma and this is my friend Jack.
steps.expected_text: my name is emma and this is my friend jack

questions.question_text: I ___ Ben. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'teacher'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Hello' is a greeting.
What does Lucy love to do? (Reading comprehension)
